title: Drawing an Informal Line of Best Fit

steps.instruction: A line of best fit is a straight line that comes as close as possible to all the data points in a scatter plot.
steps.work: The line should have roughly equal numbers of points above and below it.
steps.explanation: This line summarizes the trend and enables predictions.

steps.subgoalLabel: Pick two points on the line
steps.instruction: Choose two points that your line passes through. These don't have to be actual data points.
steps.work: The line passes through approximately (2, 15) and (8, 45).
steps.explanation: Pick points that are far apart for a more accurate slope.

steps.instruction: Find slope = (y2−y1)/(x2−x1) and use point-slope form.
steps.explanation: The equation y = 5x + 5 can now be used for predictions.

title: Using a Line of Best Fit for Predictions

steps.instruction: A line of best fit relating age (x) to height in inches (y) is y = 2.5x + 28.
steps.work: Slope = 2.5 means height increases about 2.5 inches per year.
steps.explanation: The slope describes the rate of change.

steps.instruction: Predict height at age 8 (within the data range of 5-12).
steps.explanation: Predicting within the data range (interpolation) is reliable.
Predicting far outside the data range (extrapolation) is unreliable — the trend may not continue.
